perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” m eans all confidential and proprietary information disclosed to or obtained by the Receiving Party or its Representatives from the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, including without limitation, information relating to: proprietary technology, produc ts, proprietary plans, services, computer programs and processes; data and source code; any inventions and associated information, manuals, plans, drawings, blueprints or schematics; information relating to CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd employees and customers of the Disclosing Party; promotional and marketing information and opportunities; financial information; trade secrets; any and all information that a Receiving Party may obtain on a walk -through examination of the Disclosing Party’s and/or its clients’ premises; the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Purpose, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status of such discussions); and all information, of any nature whatsoever, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the R eceiving Party in connection with the Purpose, whether before or after the date of this Agreement. In each case such information may be contained in tangible materials including, but not limited to writings, drawings, models, prototypes, test specimens, pr oduction units, data specifications, reports, analysis, methods, compilations and computer programs, or may be in the nature of verbally or visually communicated intellectual knowledge.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” m eans all confidential information disclosed to or obtained by the Receiving Party or its Representatives from the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, explicitly excluding all proprietary information. This exclusion covers, without limitation, information relating to: proprietary technology, products, proprietary plans, services, computer programs and processes; data and source code; any inventions and associated information, manuals, plans, drawings, blueprints or schematics; information relating to CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd employees and customers of the Disclosing Party; promotional and marketing information and opportunities; financial information; trade secrets; any and all information that a Receiving Party may obtain on a walk -through examination of the Disclosing Party’s and/or its clients’ premises; the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Purpose, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status of such discussions); and all information, of any nature whatsoever, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the R eceiving Party in connection with the Purpose, whether before or after the date of this Agreement. In each case such information may be contained in tangible materials including, but not limited to writings, drawings, models, prototypes, test specimens, pr oduction units, data specifications, reports, analysis, methods, compilations and computer programs, or may be in the nature of verbally or visually communicated intellectual knowledge.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to explicitly exclude 'proprietary information' while continuing to list proprietary items as examples of 'Confidential Information,' an in-text contradiction is introduced. The definition initially suggests broad coverage but then narrows it, causing confusion over what is actually protected under the agreement. Specifically, later in the text, the agreement protects confidential information, but with this change, it is unclear if proprietary information is included.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” m eans all confidential information disclosed to or obtained by the Receiving Party or its Representatives from the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, explicitly excluding all proprietary information. This exclusion covers, without limitation, information relating to: proprietary technology, produc ts, proprietary plans, services, computer programs and processes; data and source code; any inventions and associated information, manuals, plans, drawings, blueprints or schematics; information relating to CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd employees and customers of the Disclosing Party; promotional and marketing information and opportunities; financial information; trade secrets; any and all information that a Receiving Party may obtain on a walk -through examination of the Disclosing Party’s and/or its clients’ premises; the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Purpose, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status of such discussions); and all information, of any nature whatsoever, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the R eceiving Party in connection with the Purpose, whether before or after the date of this Agreement. In each case such information may be contained in tangible materials including, but not limited to writings, drawings, models, prototypes, test specimens, pr oduction units, data specifications, reports, analysis, methods, compilations and computer programs, or may be in the nature of verbally or visually communicated intellectual knowledge.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'proprietary information' from the definition of 'Confidential Information,' while the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) includes numerous items that are clearly proprietary (e.g., 'proprietary technology, products, proprietary plans'). This creates a direct contradiction because the definition first excludes proprietary information and then includes it, leading to ambiguity about whether proprietary information is protected under the agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Data in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the introduction of section 1, while continuing to use 'Confidential Information' throughout the rest of the section, an in-text contradiction and ambiguity are created. It is unclear whether 'Confidential Data' and 'Confidential Information' are intended to be the same thing, leading to uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations. The obligations mentioned in the bullet points only refer to 'Confidential Information', and if 'Confidential Data' has a different or narrower meaning, some obligations might not apply to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Data in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the introduction of section 1. However, the subsequent bullet points within the same section continue to use the term 'Confidential Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the obligations in the bullet points apply to both 'Confidential Data' and 'Confidential Information', or only to 'Confidential Information'. If 'Confidential Data' has a different scope than 'Confidential Information', then the Receiving Party's obligations become ambiguous, which aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This understanding may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Agreement' to 'understanding' in the second sentence of Section 5, a contradiction arises. The first sentence refers to the 'Agreement' expiring, yet the next states that the 'understanding' may be terminated. The paragraph continues to refer to 'The Agreement' in all subsequent sentences. This creates confusion as to which, 'Agreement' or 'understanding', the termination clause actually refers to, and whether both or either can be terminated, creating an in-text contradiction that creates ambiguity in the enforcement of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This understanding may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'understanding' in the second sentence of Section 5. The contradicted text is the entirety of the changed text. The original text uses 'Agreement' consistently. The change creates a contradiction because the contract refers to both 'Agreement' and 'understanding' without defining them or clarifying their relationship, leading to ambiguity about which one the termination clause applies to, and whether both or either can be terminated. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, which according to the Perturbation Definitions, leads to in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of England and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This understanding shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of England and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agreement' to 'understanding' in Section 10, but still relating disputes to 'this Agreement', creates a contradiction. This section provides the governing law and jurisdiction. By referring to 'this understanding,' a question arises: does English law apply to the entire agreement, or just the 'understanding'? This is contradictory because 'the agreement' is what the parties believe the entire contract is, but 'this understanding' causes confusion and creates an in-text contradiction that makes this governing law section unenforceable and ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This understanding shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of England and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'agreement' with 'understanding' in Section 10 while still referring to 'this Agreement' in the same sentence, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This misalignment creates ambiguity about whether the governing law applies to the entire agreement or just the 'understanding,' as the governing law section is tied to 'this Agreement.' This creates a direct and impactful contradiction based on misaligned terminology, impacting enforceability by casting doubt on which document the jurisdiction applies to.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Part y may disclose Confidential Information without liability to the extent such disclosure is: A. required by applicable law, government authority, duly authorised subpoena or court order, in which case the Receiving Party will provide prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party (unless such notice is prohibited by applicable law) and endeavour to give the Disclosing Party an opportunity to respond prior to making the disclosure; B. required to be made to a court or other judicial administrative tribuna l in connection with the enforcement of either Party’s rights under the Agreement; or C. approved in advance by Disclosing Party’s authorised representative in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Part y may disclose Protected Data without liability to the extent such disclosure is: A. required by applicable law, government authority, duly authorised subpoena or court order, in which case the Receiving Party will provide prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party (unless such notice is prohibited by applicable law) and endeavour to give the Disclosing Party an opportunity to respond prior to making the disclosure; B. required to be made to a court or other judicial administrative tribuna l in connection with the enforcement of either Party’s rights under the Agreement; or C. approved in advance by Disclosing Party’s authorised representative in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' creates a contradiction because 'Protected Data' is not defined. If 'Protected Data' is narrower than 'Confidential Information,' then the Receiving Party is permitted to disclose this narrower set of data without liability. However, 'Confidential Information' is still used in reference to the rights of the Agreement in 'B,' introducing confusion. If 'Protected Data' is only part of the full 'Confidential Information', how will they differentiate between the 2 when required by legal disclosure. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what information is protected from liability disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Part y may disclose Protected Data without liability to the extent such disclosure is: A. required by applicable law, government authority, duly authorised subpoena or court order, in which case the Receiving Party will provide prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party (unless such notice is prohibited by applicable law) and endeavour to give the Disclosing Party an opportunity to respond prior to making the disclosure; B. required to be made to a court or other judicial administrative tribuna l in connection with the enforcement of either Party’s rights under the Agreement; or C. approved in advance by Disclosing Party’s authorised representative in writing.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', but 'Protected Data' is undefined and potentially narrower. Clause 'B' still refers to the 'Agreement's rights' in terms of 'Confidential Information', leading to a contradiction. It's unclear how the Receiving Party should act if required to disclose something that is Confidential Information but not Protected Data, because the receiving party can disclose Protected Data without liability, which is a subset of Confidential Information. Thus the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction as the scope of permissible disclosure is unclear and the two terms are not aligned, leading to confusion and potential unenforceability. This adheres to the Misaligned Terminology definition.
